No Poverty.reason: The course on financial markets and investment decisions indirectly relates to Target 1.4 by providing knowledge on financial services, which can aid economic resource access for the vulnerable.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course covers "futures," which can include food commodity derivatives, indirectly relating to Target 2.c on the proper functioning of food commodity markets.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No evidence in the course description or topics directly or indirectly relates to good health and well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly aims to provide "practical skills that can be applied in investment decisions," directly contributing to Target 4.4 on relevant skills for employment.
evidence: practical skills that can be applied in investment decisions

Gender Equality.reason: No evidence in the course description or topics directly or indirectly relates to gender equality.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the course description or topics directly or indirectly relates to affordable and clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly supports Target 8.10 by focusing on financial markets and institutions, and Target 8.3 by promoting productive activities through investment decisions.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on financial markets and equity valuation directly relates to Target 9.3, which aims to increase enterprises' access to financial services for innovation.
evidence: valuation of equity and fixed-income securities

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Understanding financial markets, a core course topic, is foundational for improving their regulation and monitoring (Target 10.5) to reduce inequalities.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence in the course description or topics directly or indirectly relates to life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Knowledge of financial markets is foundational for understanding and addressing illicit financial flows (Target 16.4) and developing transparent financial institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course directly addresses mobilizing financial resources (Target 17.3) and enhancing global macroeconomic stability (Target 17.13) through its focus on financial markets and investment theories.
